_note: SYNTHETIC DEMO FIXTURE — not investment advice, not real CAVA disclosures. Numbers are illustrative and the 'Meridian Securities' note is fictional. This is the pre-call structured input for the Diligence-Call Copilot demo: the fund's question list + thesis + the modeled assumptions the copilot grounds follow-ups and contradiction flags against.
thesis: CAVA is an early-innings unit-growth compounder: a defensible Mediterranean category lead with best-in-class AUVs (~$2.9M) and ~25% restaurant-level margins, a long runway to ~1,000 units, and traffic-led same-restaurant sales. The bet is that new-unit economics and comp quality hold as the footprint moves beyond the coastal core.
thesis_memo: We are long CAVA on the unit-growth runway (367 units today -> ~1,000 by 2032) at industry-leading new-unit returns. The thesis rests on two load-bearing assumptions we need the sell-side analyst to defend on this call: (1) new-vintage units keep opening near the modeled ~$2.6M AUV at ~20% year-one restaurant-level margin, and (2) same-restaurant sales stay TRAFFIC-led, not price-led — price-reliant comp is lower quality and signals less runway. If either cracks, the multiple is not supportable.

modeled_assumptions.fy26_unit_growth: ~17% (~65 net new), fully covered by signed pipeline

questions.text: How derisked is the FY26 ~17% new-unit guide? What's signed-lease coverage and how deep is the development pipeline beyond the next twelve months?
questions.why: Unit growth is the whole thesis. If the pipeline is thin or leases aren't signed, the compounding story slips.
questions.demo_role: clean tick — answered early and cleanly

questions.text: Are recent-vintage new units opening at the modeled ~$2.6M AUV, and how are the 2023-24 cohorts maturing on restaurant-level margin specifically?
questions.why: New-unit economics are the second load-bearing assumption. A vague 'they're tracking well' is not enough — we need the AUV and the year-one restaurant-level margin on the actual recent class, because that is what underwrites the ~1,000-unit math.
questions.demo_role: HERO — researcher answers vaguely -> stays partial/amber -> copilot fires the grounded follow-up -> closes to green

questions.text: Can the ~25% fleet restaurant-level margin hold through wage inflation, the steak/protein mix shift, and new-unit drag?
questions.why: Margin durability underpins the cash generation that self-funds growth.

questions.text: What's the traffic vs price/mix split in current same-restaurant sales, and is traffic still positive?
questions.why: Comp QUALITY is the differentiator. The note frames CAVA's comp as traffic-led; if it's actually price-led, the runway and the premium multiple are weaker than underwritten.
questions.demo_role: INCONSISTENCY — answer contradicts the note's traffic-led model; flag fires vs 'note'. Question still reaches answered.

questions.text: Where does the loyalty relaunch stand, what's the digital sales mix, and what frequency lift are you seeing?
questions.why: Loyalty + digital drive frequency, which is the cleaner (traffic) lever for comp.

questions.text: How defensible is the Mediterranean category lead versus Chipotle, Sweetgreen, and new entrants — and how much pricing power is left?
questions.why: Moat and pricing power determine whether the AUV/margin premium persists as the category attracts capital.

questions.text: What's the read on the catering rollout and any smaller-format or new-daypart prototype economics?
questions.why: Optionality on the growth algorithm — incremental sales layers beyond the core box.
questions.demo_role: late grey -> green — stays unanswered until the copilot nudges near the end of the call, then the analyst raises it and it closes

questions.text: Is the unit growth self-funded from operating cash flow, or is a raise needed — and is there any M&A or buyback appetite?
questions.why: Funding risk. Dilution or a stretched balance sheet changes the return profile.
